title: agenui_catalog A2UI Catalog
description: agenui_catalog A2UI catalog, including basic_catalog, common_types.

allOf.description: Text component for displaying plain text or simple Markdown text. Use 'variant' to apply preset font styles (h1~h5/caption/body); supports text-decoration properties from TextStyles.

text.description: The text content to display. While simple Markdown formatting is supported (i.e. without HTML, images, or links), utilizing dedicated UI components is generally preferred for a richer and more structured presentation.

variant.description: A hint for the base text style.

allOf.description: Image component supporting asynchronous network image loading, multiple fit modes and preset size variants. Has a default 1px border (#0000001A); use 'variant' to quickly apply common image styles.

url.description: The URL of the image to display. Supports http/https URLs, local resources (res://name), and file paths (file:///path).

fit.description: Specifies how the image should be resized to fit its container. This corresponds to the CSS 'object-fit' property.

variant.description: A hint for the image size and style. icon: 48x48px; avatar: 88x88px circle; smallFeature: 100% width with 4:3 ratio; mediumFeature: 100% width with 3:2 ratio; largeFeature: 100% width with 16:9 ratio; header: 100% width with 16:9 ratio and no rounded corners.

name.description: The name of the icon to display.

url.description: The URL of the video to display.
The URL of the audio to be played.

description.description: A description of the audio, such as a title or summary.

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children horizontally. To create a grid layout, nest Columns within this Row.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (horizontally). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges, or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (vertically). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property, but uses camelCase values (e.g., 'start').

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children vertically. To create a grid layout, nest Rows within this Column.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (vertically). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges (e.g. header at top, footer at bottom), or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (horizontally). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list.

direction.description: The direction in which the list items are laid out.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis.

child.description: The ID of the single child component to be rendered inside the card. To display multiple elements, you MUST wrap them in a layout component (like Column or Row) and pass that container's ID here. Do NOT pass multiple IDs or a non-existent ID. Do NOT define the child component inline.

tabs.description: An array of objects, where each object defines a tab with a title and a child component.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Do NOT define the component inline.

trigger.description: The ID of the component that opens the modal when interacted with (e.g., a button). Do NOT define the component inline.

content.description: The ID of the component to be displayed inside the modal. Do NOT define the component inline.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Use a 'Text' component for a labeled button. Only use an 'Icon' if the requirements explicitly ask for an icon-only button. Do NOT define the child component inline.

variant.description: A hint for the button style. If omitted, a default button style is used. 'primary' indicates this is the main call-to-action button. 'borderless' means the button has no visual border or background, making its child content appear like a clickable link.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

value.description: The value of the text field.

variant.description: The type of input field to display.

validationRegexp.description: A regular expression used for client-side validation of the input.

label.description: The text to display next to the checkbox.

value.description: The current state of the checkbox (true for checked, false for unchecked).

allOf.description: A component that allows selecting one or more options from a list.

label.description: The label for the group of options.

variant.description: A hint for how the choice picker should be displayed and behave.

options.description: The list of available options to choose from.

label.description: The text to display for this option.

value.description: The stable value associated with this option.
The list of currently selected values. This should be bound to a string array in the data model.

displayStyle.description: The display style of the component.

filterable.description: If true, displays a search input to filter the options.

min.description: The minimum value of the slider.

max.description: The maximum value of the slider.

value.description: The current value of the slider.
The selected date and/or time value in ISO 8601 format. If not yet set, initialize with an empty string.

enableDate.description: If true, allows the user to select a date.

enableTime.description: If true, allows the user to select a time.

min.description: The minimum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

max.description: The maximum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

allOf.description: Rich-text component that renders HTML-formatted content. Supports common HTML tags (b/i/u/a/img/font/p/br/ul/ol/li/h1~h6, etc.); use 'variant' to set the base font style, and link clicking can be toggled.

text.description:
Rich-text content in HTML format. Supported tags include: , , , , , , , , , ,

,
,

    ,
      ,
    1. ,

      ~

      , etc.

variant.description: Text style hint used to set the base font size and style.

linksEnable.description: Whether links are clickable. Defaults to true; when set to false, links cannot be clicked.

allOf.description: Lottie animation component that loads and plays Lottie JSON animations. Supports network URLs (http/https), bundled resources (res://name) and local files (file:///path). Looping, auto-play and size variants (icon/small/medium/large) are configurable.

url.description: URL of the Lottie JSON animation file. Supports network URLs (http/https), bundled resources (res://animation_name) and local files (file:///path).

loop.description: Whether the animation loops. Defaults to true.

autoPlay.description: Whether the animation auto-plays. Defaults to true.

allOf.description: Table component that displays structured data in rows and columns. Has a default 1px border and 16px rounded corners; cell-padding and column-weights can be customized through styles.

columns.description: Array of column names defining the table headers.

rows.description: Array of table data rows. Each row is an array of strings containing the cell values for that row.

allOf.description: Embedded web component that renders the specified URL inside a WebView. Supports adaptive height (min-height: 240px, max-height: 1600px) and automatically adjusts to the content size.

url.description: Web page URL. Accepts a full http/https URL. The WebView will automatically load and display the page, with JavaScript and adaptive height supported.

allOf.description: Markdown rendering component that renders Markdown text as native rich text. Supports the standard syntax (headings, bold, italic, lists, links, images, code blocks, blockquotes, tables, etc.) and incremental streaming content.

content.description: Markdown-formatted text content. Implemented with the Markwon library and supports standard Markdown syntax: headings (# H1, ## H2), bold (**bold**), italic (*italic*), lists (- item), links ([text](url)), images (![alt](url)), code (`code`), blockquotes (> quote), tables, etc. Supports incremental streaming append.

allOf.description: Image carousel component that lets users swipe horizontally through multiple images. Includes a built-in pill-shaped page indicator, and supports auto-play and gesture-driven switching.

content.description: Array of image URLs for the carousel. Supports network URLs (http/https), bundled resources (res://name) and local files (file:///path).

autoplay.description: Whether the carousel auto-plays. Defaults to false. When enabled, slides advance at the autoplaySpeed interval; auto-play pauses while dragging and resumes after release.

draggable.description: Whether users can drag to switch images manually. Defaults to true.

allOf.description: Chart component supporting multiple chart types (line/donut/bar). Renders structured chart data with optional axis labels and series. Color palette can be customized via styles.chartConfig.colors.

data.description: Chart data structure containing axis labels and one or more series.

xAxis.description: Optional X-axis labels (used by line/bar charts).

yAxis.description: Optional Y-axis labels (used by line/bar charts).

series.description: Array of data series. For donut charts a single series with labeled entries is used; for line/bar charts each series represents one line or one set of bars.

name.description: Series display name shown in legends.

data.description: Array of data points for this series.

value.description: Numeric value of this data point.

label.description: Optional label for this data point (used by donut charts as slice label).

required.description: Checks that the value is not null, undefined, or empty.

value.description: The value to check.

regex.description: Checks that the value matches a regular expression string.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to match against.

email.description: Checks that the value is a valid email address.

formatString.description: Performs string interpolation of data model values and other functions in the catalog functions list and returns the resulting string. The value string can contain interpolated expressions in the `${expression}` format. Supported expression types include: JSON Pointer paths to the data model (e.g., `${/absolute/path}` or `${relative/path}`), and client-side function calls (e.g., `${now()}`). Function arguments must be named (e.g., `${formatDate(value:${/currentDate}, format:'MM-dd')}`). To include a literal `${` sequence, escape it as `\${`.

formatNumber.description: Formats a number with the specified grouping and decimal precision.

value.description: The number to format.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

formatCurrency.description: Formats a number as a currency string.

value.description: The monetary amount.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

formatDate.description: Formats a timestamp into a string using a pattern.

value.description: The date to format.

format.description:
A Unicode TR35 date pattern string.

Token Reference:
- Year: 'yy' (26), 'yyyy' (2026)
- Month: 'M' (1), 'MM' (01), 'MMM' (Jan), 'MMMM' (January)
- Day: 'd' (1), 'dd' (01), 'E' (Tue), 'EEEE' (Tuesday)
- Hour (12h): 'h' (1-12), 'hh' (01-12) - requires 'a' for AM/PM
- Hour (24h): 'H' (0-23), 'HH' (00-23) - Military Time
- Minute: 'mm' (00-59)
- Second: 'ss' (00-59)
- Period: 'a' (AM/PM)

Examples:
- 'MMM dd, yyyy' -> 'Jan 16, 2026'
- 'HH:mm' -> '14:30' (Military)
- 'h:mm a' -> '2:30 PM'
- 'EEEE, d MMMM' -> 'Friday, 16 January'

pluralize.description: Returns a localized string based on the Common Locale Data Repository (CLDR) plural category of the count (zero, one, two, few, many, other). Requires an 'other' fallback. For English, just use 'one' and 'other'.

value.description: The numeric value used to determine the plural category.

two.description: String for the 'two' category (used in Arabic, Welsh, etc.).

few.description: String for the 'few' category (e.g., small groups in Slavic languages).

many.description: String for the 'many' category (e.g., large groups in various languages).

other.description: The default/fallback string (used for general plural cases).

openUrl.description: Opens the specified URL in a browser or handler. This function has no return value.

token.description: Parses a token reference and resolves it to its actual value from the loaded design tokens. The resolver automatically selects the light or dark value based on the current theme mode (falls back to the other mode if the active one is missing). Returns the original token name if no matching token is found.

name.description: The design token name to resolve, e.g., 'primaryColor', 'spacing.md'.

and.description: Performs a logical AND operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

or.description: Performs a logical OR operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

not.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on a boolean value.

value.description: The boolean value to negate.

common_types_ComponentId.description: The unique identifier for a component, used for both definitions and references within the same surface.

width.description: Specifies the width of the component. Accepted formats: 1) length in px (e.g. 100px); 2) percentage (e.g. 50%); 3) 'auto' to size to content.

height.description: Specifies the height of the component. Accepted formats: 1) length in px (e.g. 100px); 2) percentage (e.g. 50%); 3) 'auto' to size to content.

min-width.description: Specifies the minimum width. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 100px or 50%.

max-width.description: Specifies the maximum width. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 600px or 100%.

min-height.description: Specifies the minimum height. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 100px or 50%.

max-height.description: Specifies the maximum height. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 600px or 100%.

margin.description: Shorthand for the four margin longhands. Accepts 1 to 4 values: 1 value = all sides; 2 values = top/bottom | left/right; 3 values = top | left/right | bottom; 4 values = top | right | bottom | left. E.g. 10px, 10px 20px, 10px 20px 10px 20px.

margin-top.description: Specifies the top margin. Accepts a length in px or 'auto', e.g. 10px.

margin-right.description: Specifies the right margin. Accepts a length in px or 'auto', e.g. 10px.

margin-bottom.description: Specifies the bottom margin. Accepts a length in px or 'auto', e.g. 10px.

margin-left.description: Specifies the left margin. Accepts a length in px or 'auto', e.g. 10px.

padding.description: Shorthand for the four padding longhands. Accepts 1 to 4 values: 1 value = all sides; 2 values = top/bottom | left/right; 3 values = top | left/right | bottom; 4 values = top | right | bottom | left. E.g. 10px, 10px 20px, 10px 20px 10px 20px.

padding-top.description: Specifies the top padding. Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-right.description: Specifies the right padding. Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-bottom.description: Specifies the bottom padding. Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-left.description: Specifies the left padding. Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

display.description: Specifies the box layout type. 'flex' establishes a flex formatting context (default); 'none' removes the element from the layout.

visibility.description: Specifies whether the element is rendered. 'visible' is rendered; 'hidden' is invisible but still occupies layout space.

opacity.description: Specifies the opacity of the element. Values range from 0 (fully transparent) to 1 (fully opaque).

overflow.description: Specifies how content that overflows the box is handled. 'hidden' clips overflow; 'visible' renders overflow outside the box; 'scroll' enables scrolling to view overflow.

flex-direction.description: Specifies the main axis direction of the flex container. 'row' is horizontal left-to-right; 'row-reverse' is horizontal right-to-left; 'column' is vertical top-to-bottom; 'column-reverse' is vertical bottom-to-top.

flex-wrap.description: Specifies whether flex items wrap onto multiple lines. 'nowrap' keeps items on a single line; 'wrap' allows wrapping; 'wrap-reverse' wraps in the reverse direction.

justify-content.description: Specifies the alignment of flex items along the main axis. 'flex-start' packs items to the start; 'flex-end' packs items to the end; 'center' centers items; 'space-between' distributes items with the first and last at the edges; 'space-around' distributes items with equal space around each; 'space-evenly' distributes items with equal space between and around.

align-items.description: Specifies the default alignment of flex items along the cross axis. 'flex-start' aligns to the start; 'flex-end' aligns to the end; 'center' centers items; 'stretch' fills the cross axis; 'baseline' aligns to the items' baselines.

align-self.description: Overrides the parent container's 'align-items' value for an individual flex item along the cross axis.

align-content.description: Specifies the alignment of flex lines along the cross axis when extra space is present. Only effective when 'flex-wrap' is enabled.

flex-grow.description: Specifies the flex grow factor. Defaults to 0 (no growth). A positive value distributes remaining space proportionally.

flex-shrink.description: Specifies the flex shrink factor. Defaults to 1 (items shrink to fit). A value of 0 prevents shrinking.

flex-basis.description: Specifies the initial main size of a flex item. Accepts a length in px, a percentage, or 'auto', e.g. 200px, 50%, auto.

position.description: Specifies the positioning scheme. 'relative' positions the element relative to its normal position (default); 'absolute' positions the element relative to its nearest positioned ancestor.

top.description: Specifies the top offset for a positioned element. Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 10px, 5%.

right.description: Specifies the right offset for a positioned element. Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 10px, 5%.

bottom.description: Specifies the bottom offset for a positioned element. Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 10px, 5%.

left.description: Specifies the left offset for a positioned element. Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage, e.g. 10px, 5%.

aspect-ratio.description: Specifies the preferred aspect ratio of the box. Accepts a single number (e.g. 1.5) or a ratio (e.g. 16/9 or 4 / 3). When set, the height is computed from the width.

background-color.description: Specifies the background color or gradient. Accepted formats: 1) hexadecimal #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA (e.g. #ff0000 or #ff000080); 2) rgb()/rgba() functional notation (e.g. rgb(255,0,0) or rgba(255,99,71,0.5)); 3) linear-gradient (e.g. linear-gradient(180deg, #FF0000 0%, #0000FF 100%)); 4) radial-gradient (e.g. radial-gradient(circle at center, #ff0000, #0000ff)); 5) conic-gradient (e.g. conic-gradient(from 0deg, #ff0000, #0000ff)).

background-image.description: Specifies a background image. Accepts the url() functional notation, e.g. url('https://example.com/bg.png'). The image is loaded asynchronously and fills the component's box.

border-radius.description: Specifies the border corner radius. Accepts a length in px (e.g. 10px) and applies to all four corners.

border-width.description: Specifies the border width. Accepts a length in px, e.g. 1px.

border-color.description: Specifies the border color. Accepted formats: 1) hexadecimal #RRGGBB (e.g. #ff0000); 2) hexadecimal with alpha #RRGGBBAA (e.g. #ff000080); 3) rgb() functional notation (e.g. rgb(255, 0, 0)); 4) rgba() functional notation (e.g. rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)).

border-style.description: Specifies the border line style. Only 'solid' is supported.

filter.description: Specifies a graphical effect applied to the element. Only the drop-shadow() function is supported. Format: drop-shadow(offset-x offset-y blur-radius color), e.g. drop-shadow(0px 4px 4px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.25)).

margin-block-start.description: Logical property: block-start margin (typically maps to the top margin). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

margin-block-end.description: Logical property: block-end margin (typically maps to the bottom margin). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

margin-inline-start.description: Logical property: inline-start margin (maps to the left margin in LTR, the right margin in RTL). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

margin-inline-end.description: Logical property: inline-end margin (maps to the right margin in LTR, the left margin in RTL). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-block-start.description: Logical property: block-start padding (typically maps to the top padding). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-block-end.description: Logical property: block-end padding (typically maps to the bottom padding). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-inline-start.description: Logical property: inline-start padding (maps to the left padding in LTR, the right padding in RTL). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

padding-inline-end.description: Logical property: inline-end padding (maps to the right padding in LTR, the left padding in RTL). Accepts a length in px, e.g. 10px.

inset-inline-start.description: Logical property: inline-start offset (maps to 'left' in LTR, 'right' in RTL). Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage.

inset-inline-end.description: Logical property: inline-end offset (maps to 'right' in LTR, 'left' in RTL). Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage.

inset-block-start.description: Logical property: block-start offset (typically maps to 'top'). Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage.

inset-block-end.description: Logical property: block-end offset (typically maps to 'bottom'). Used together with 'position'. Accepts a length in px or a percentage.

common_types_Styles.description: Common style properties controlling the appearance and layout of a component. Defines a CSS subset implemented with the Yoga engine for Flexbox layout. Length values use px (the unit suffix may be omitted; '10' and '10px' are equivalent) and are converted at render time according to screen density. Selected properties also accept percentages and 'auto'.

path.description: A JSON Pointer path to a value in the data model.

common_types_DynamicValue.description: A value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning any type.

common_types_FunctionCall.description: Invokes a named function on the client.

call.description: The name of the function to call.

returnType.description: The expected return type of the function call.

common_types_AccessibilityAttributes.description: Attributes to enhance accessibility when using assistive technologies like screen readers.

label.description: A short string, typically 1 to 3 words, used by assistive technologies to convey the purpose or intent of an element. For example, an input field might have an accessible label of 'User ID' or a button might be labeled 'Submit'.

description.description: Additional information provided by assistive technologies about an element such as instructions, format requirements, or result of an action. For example, a mute button might have a label of 'Mute' and a description of 'Silences notifications about this conversation'.

styles.description: Style properties applied to the component.

color.description: Specifies the text color. Accepted formats: 1) hexadecimal #RRGGBB; 2) hexadecimal with alpha #RRGGBBAA; 3) rgb() functional notation (e.g. rgb(255, 0, 0)); 4) rgba() functional notation (e.g. rgba(255, 99, 71, 0.5)).

font-size.description: Specifies the font size. Only px is accepted (e.g. 16px). The value is converted at render time according to screen density (iOS: x0.5; Android: /2 x density).

line-clamp.description: Specifies the maximum number of visible text lines. A value less than or equal to 0 means unlimited (default 0). A positive value truncates text exceeding that line count; combine with 'text-overflow' to display an ellipsis.

text-align.description: Specifies the horizontal and optional vertical alignment of text. 'left top' = top-left; 'left' / 'left center' = left, vertically centered; 'left bottom' = bottom-left; 'center top' = horizontally centered, top-aligned; 'center' / 'center center' = horizontally and vertically centered; 'center bottom' = horizontally centered, bottom-aligned; 'right top' = top-right; 'right' / 'right center' = right, vertically centered; 'right bottom' = bottom-right.

font-family.description: Specifies the font family. Use 'system' for the system default, or a font family name for custom fonts (e.g. system, Arial, PingFang SC).

font-weight.description: Specifies the font weight. 'normal' is regular weight (400); 'bold' is bold weight (700). On Android, only the binary 'normal' / 'bold' values are supported.

oneOf.description: Unitless line-height as a multiple of the font size, e.g. 1.5 means 1.5 x font-size.
Absolute line-height in px, e.g. 40px. For multi-line text, the unitless form is recommended for cross-platform consistency.

line-height.description: Specifies the line height controlling vertical spacing between lines. Accepts either: 1) a unitless multiplier (recommended, e.g. 1.5); or 2) an absolute length in px (e.g. 40px). All three platforms use a centered distribution algorithm to match the W3C line-height semantics.

text-overflow.description: Specifies how clipped inline content is signaled. Must be combined with 'line-clamp'. 'clip' truncates without an indicator (default); 'ellipsis' shows a trailing ellipsis (...); 'head' shows a leading ellipsis (requires line-clamp = 1); 'middle' shows the ellipsis in the middle (requires line-clamp = 1).

text-decoration.description: Shorthand text decoration property. Format: [line] [style] [color], e.g. underline dashed #FF0000. 'line' = underline | line-through; 'style' = solid | dashed | dotted | double | wavy; 'color' = a color value.

text-decoration-line.description: Specifies the kind of decoration line. 'none' = no decoration; 'underline' = underline; 'line-through' = strikethrough.

text-decoration-color.description: Specifies the decoration line color. Accepts hexadecimal #RRGGBB or #RRGGBBAA. Defaults to the text color when unspecified.

text-decoration-style.description: Specifies the style of the decoration line. 'solid' is the default; 'dashed', 'dotted', 'double', and 'wavy' are also supported.

text-decoration-thickness.description: Specifies the thickness of the decoration line. Only px is accepted (e.g. 2px). Defaults to 1px.

common_types_TextStyles.description: Style properties for text components. Inherits all common Styles properties and extends them with text-specific typography (font, color, alignment, line-height, decoration, etc.).

basic_catalog_TextComponentCommon.description: Common properties for Text component, with extended text-specific styles.

styles.description: Style properties for the Text component, including the common styles and text-specific properties.

weight.description: The relative weight of this component within a Row or Column. This is similar to the CSS 'flex-grow' property. Note: this may ONLY be set when the component is a direct descendant of a Row or Column.

oneOf.description: A static list of child component IDs.
A template for generating a dynamic list of children from a data model list. The `componentId` is the component to use as a template.

path.description: The path to the list of component property objects in the data model.

common_types_DynamicBoolean.description: A boolean value that can be a literal, a path, or a function call returning a boolean.

common_types_CheckRule.description: A single validation rule applied to an input component.

message.description: The error message to display if the check fails.

common_types_Checkable.description: Properties for components that support client-side checks.

checks.description: A list of checks to perform. These are function calls that must return a boolean indicating validity.

common_types_Action.description: Defines an interaction handler that can either trigger a server-side event or execute a local client-side function.

event.description: The event to dispatch to the server.

name.description: The name of the action to be dispatched to the server.

context.description: A JSON object containing the key-value pairs for the action context. Values can be literals or paths. Use literal values unless the value must be dynamically bound to the data model. Do NOT use paths for static IDs.

common_types_DynamicStringList.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal array of strings, a path to a string array in the data model, or a function call returning a string array.

common_types_DynamicNumber.description: Represents a value that can be either a literal number, a path to a number in the data model, or a function call returning a number.

allOf.description: Text component for displaying plain text or simple Markdown text. Use 'variant' to apply preset font styles (h1~h5/caption/body); supports text-decoration properties from TextStyles.

text.description: The text content to display. While simple Markdown formatting is supported (i.e. without HTML, images, or links), utilizing dedicated UI components is generally preferred for a richer and more structured presentation.

variant.description: A hint for the base text style.

allOf.description: Image component supporting asynchronous network image loading, multiple fit modes and preset size variants. Has a default 1px border (#0000001A); use 'variant' to quickly apply common image styles.

url.description: The URL of the image to display. Supports http/https URLs, local resources (res://name), and file paths (file:///path).

fit.description: Specifies how the image should be resized to fit its container. This corresponds to the CSS 'object-fit' property.

variant.description: A hint for the image size and style. icon: 48x48px; avatar: 88x88px circle; smallFeature: 100% width with 4:3 ratio; mediumFeature: 100% width with 3:2 ratio; largeFeature: 100% width with 16:9 ratio; header: 100% width with 16:9 ratio and no rounded corners.

name.description: The name of the icon to display.

url.description: The URL of the video to display.
The URL of the audio to be played.

description.description: A description of the audio, such as a title or summary.

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children horizontally. To create a grid layout, nest Columns within this Row.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (horizontally). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges, or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (vertically). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property, but uses camelCase values (e.g., 'start').

allOf.description: A layout component that arranges its children vertically. To create a grid layout, nest Rows within this Column.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list. Children cannot be defined inline, they must be referred to by ID.

justify.description: Defines the arrangement of children along the main axis (vertically). Use 'spaceBetween' to push items to the edges (e.g. header at top, footer at bottom), or 'start'/'end'/'center' to pack them together.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis (horizontally). This is similar to the CSS 'align-items' property.

children.description: Defines the children. Use an array of strings for a fixed set of children, or a template object to generate children from a data list.

direction.description: The direction in which the list items are laid out.

align.description: Defines the alignment of children along the cross axis.

child.description: The ID of the single child component to be rendered inside the card. To display multiple elements, you MUST wrap them in a layout component (like Column or Row) and pass that container's ID here. Do NOT pass multiple IDs or a non-existent ID. Do NOT define the child component inline.

tabs.description: An array of objects, where each object defines a tab with a title and a child component.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Do NOT define the component inline.

trigger.description: The ID of the component that opens the modal when interacted with (e.g., a button). Do NOT define the component inline.

content.description: The ID of the component to be displayed inside the modal. Do NOT define the component inline.

child.description: The ID of the child component. Use a 'Text' component for a labeled button. Only use an 'Icon' if the requirements explicitly ask for an icon-only button. Do NOT define the child component inline.

variant.description: A hint for the button style. If omitted, a default button style is used. 'primary' indicates this is the main call-to-action button. 'borderless' means the button has no visual border or background, making its child content appear like a clickable link.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

value.description: The value of the text field.

variant.description: The type of input field to display.

validationRegexp.description: A regular expression used for client-side validation of the input.

label.description: The text to display next to the checkbox.

value.description: The current state of the checkbox (true for checked, false for unchecked).

allOf.description: A component that allows selecting one or more options from a list.

label.description: The label for the group of options.

variant.description: A hint for how the choice picker should be displayed and behave.

options.description: The list of available options to choose from.

label.description: The text to display for this option.

value.description: The stable value associated with this option.
The list of currently selected values. This should be bound to a string array in the data model.

displayStyle.description: The display style of the component.

filterable.description: If true, displays a search input to filter the options.

min.description: The minimum value of the slider.

max.description: The maximum value of the slider.

value.description: The current value of the slider.
The selected date and/or time value in ISO 8601 format. If not yet set, initialize with an empty string.

enableDate.description: If true, allows the user to select a date.

enableTime.description: If true, allows the user to select a time.

min.description: The minimum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

max.description: The maximum allowed date/time in ISO 8601 format.

label.description: The text label for the input field.

allOf.description: Rich-text component that renders HTML-formatted content. Supports common HTML tags (b/i/u/a/img/font/p/br/ul/ol/li/h1~h6, etc.); use 'variant' to set the base font style, and link clicking can be toggled.

text.description:
Rich-text content in HTML format. Supported tags include: , , , , , , , , , ,

,
,

    ,
      ,
    1. ,

      ~

      , etc.

variant.description: Text style hint used to set the base font size and style.

linksEnable.description: Whether links are clickable. Defaults to true; when set to false, links cannot be clicked.

allOf.description: Lottie animation component that loads and plays Lottie JSON animations. Supports network URLs (http/https), bundled resources (res://name) and local files (file:///path). Looping, auto-play and size variants (icon/small/medium/large) are configurable.

url.description: URL of the Lottie JSON animation file. Supports network URLs (http/https), bundled resources (res://animation_name) and local files (file:///path).

loop.description: Whether the animation loops. Defaults to true.

autoPlay.description: Whether the animation auto-plays. Defaults to true.

allOf.description: Table component that displays structured data in rows and columns. Has a default 1px border and 16px rounded corners; cell-padding and column-weights can be customized through styles.

columns.description: Array of column names defining the table headers.

rows.description: Array of table data rows. Each row is an array of strings containing the cell values for that row.

allOf.description: Embedded web component that renders the specified URL inside a WebView. Supports adaptive height (min-height: 240px, max-height: 1600px) and automatically adjusts to the content size.

url.description: Web page URL. Accepts a full http/https URL. The WebView will automatically load and display the page, with JavaScript and adaptive height supported.

allOf.description: Markdown rendering component that renders Markdown text as native rich text. Supports the standard syntax (headings, bold, italic, lists, links, images, code blocks, blockquotes, tables, etc.) and incremental streaming content.

content.description: Markdown-formatted text content. Implemented with the Markwon library and supports standard Markdown syntax: headings (# H1, ## H2), bold (**bold**), italic (*italic*), lists (- item), links ([text](url)), images (![alt](url)), code (`code`), blockquotes (> quote), tables, etc. Supports incremental streaming append.

allOf.description: Image carousel component that lets users swipe horizontally through multiple images. Includes a built-in pill-shaped page indicator, and supports auto-play and gesture-driven switching.

content.description: Array of image URLs for the carousel. Supports network URLs (http/https), bundled resources (res://name) and local files (file:///path).

autoplay.description: Whether the carousel auto-plays. Defaults to false. When enabled, slides advance at the autoplaySpeed interval; auto-play pauses while dragging and resumes after release.

draggable.description: Whether users can drag to switch images manually. Defaults to true.

allOf.description: Chart component supporting multiple chart types (line/donut/bar). Renders structured chart data with optional axis labels and series. Color palette can be customized via styles.chartConfig.colors.

data.description: Chart data structure containing axis labels and one or more series.

xAxis.description: Optional X-axis labels (used by line/bar charts).

yAxis.description: Optional Y-axis labels (used by line/bar charts).

series.description: Array of data series. For donut charts a single series with labeled entries is used; for line/bar charts each series represents one line or one set of bars.

name.description: Series display name shown in legends.

data.description: Array of data points for this series.

value.description: Numeric value of this data point.

label.description: Optional label for this data point (used by donut charts as slice label).

basic_catalog_required.description: Checks that the value is not null, undefined, or empty.

value.description: The value to check.

basic_catalog_regex.description: Checks that the value matches a regular expression string.

pattern.description: The regex pattern to match against.

basic_catalog_email.description: Checks that the value is a valid email address.

basic_catalog_formatString.description: Performs string interpolation of data model values and other functions in the catalog functions list and returns the resulting string. The value string can contain interpolated expressions in the `${expression}` format. Supported expression types include: JSON Pointer paths to the data model (e.g., `${/absolute/path}` or `${relative/path}`), and client-side function calls (e.g., `${now()}`). Function arguments must be named (e.g., `${formatDate(value:${/currentDate}, format:'MM-dd')}`). To include a literal `${` sequence, escape it as `\${`.

basic_catalog_formatNumber.description: Formats a number with the specified grouping and decimal precision.

value.description: The number to format.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

basic_catalog_formatCurrency.description: Formats a number as a currency string.

value.description: The monetary amount.

decimals.description: Optional. The number of decimal places to show. Defaults to 0 or 2 depending on locale.

grouping.description: Optional. If true, uses locale-specific grouping separators (e.g. '1,000'). If false, returns raw digits (e.g. '1000'). Defaults to true.

basic_catalog_formatDate.description: Formats a timestamp into a string using a pattern.

value.description: The date to format.

format.description:
A Unicode TR35 date pattern string.

Token Reference:
- Year: 'yy' (26), 'yyyy' (2026)
- Month: 'M' (1), 'MM' (01), 'MMM' (Jan), 'MMMM' (January)
- Day: 'd' (1), 'dd' (01), 'E' (Tue), 'EEEE' (Tuesday)
- Hour (12h): 'h' (1-12), 'hh' (01-12) - requires 'a' for AM/PM
- Hour (24h): 'H' (0-23), 'HH' (00-23) - Military Time
- Minute: 'mm' (00-59)
- Second: 'ss' (00-59)
- Period: 'a' (AM/PM)

Examples:
- 'MMM dd, yyyy' -> 'Jan 16, 2026'
- 'HH:mm' -> '14:30' (Military)
- 'h:mm a' -> '2:30 PM'
- 'EEEE, d MMMM' -> 'Friday, 16 January'

basic_catalog_pluralize.description: Returns a localized string based on the Common Locale Data Repository (CLDR) plural category of the count (zero, one, two, few, many, other). Requires an 'other' fallback. For English, just use 'one' and 'other'.

value.description: The numeric value used to determine the plural category.

two.description: String for the 'two' category (used in Arabic, Welsh, etc.).

few.description: String for the 'few' category (e.g., small groups in Slavic languages).

many.description: String for the 'many' category (e.g., large groups in various languages).

other.description: The default/fallback string (used for general plural cases).

basic_catalog_openUrl.description: Opens the specified URL in a browser or handler. This function has no return value.

basic_catalog_token.description: Parses a token reference and resolves it to its actual value from the loaded design tokens. The resolver automatically selects the light or dark value based on the current theme mode (falls back to the other mode if the active one is missing). Returns the original token name if no matching token is found.

name.description: The design token name to resolve, e.g., 'primaryColor', 'spacing.md'.

basic_catalog_and.description: Performs a logical AND operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

basic_catalog_or.description: Performs a logical OR operation on a list of boolean values.

values.description: The list of boolean values to evaluate.

basic_catalog_not.description: Performs a logical NOT operation on a boolean value.

value.description: The boolean value to negate.

primaryColor.description: The primary brand color used for highlights (e.g., primary buttons, active borders). Renderers may generate variants of this color for different contexts. Format: Hexadecimal code (e.g., '#00BFFF').

iconUrl.description: A URL for an image that identifies the agent or tool associated with the surface.

agentDisplayName.description: Text to be displayed next to the surface to identify the agent or tool that created it.
